title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket is for sale for $12.5 million as owners Evan and Maria Marley retire after 25 years.
The sale includes the restaurant at 11 West Creek Road and staff housing located at 41.5 Nobadeer Farm Road.
The pizzeria will continue operations and is hiring, with Evan Marley remaining temporarily to ensure a smooth ownership transition.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: France's national soccer team left Boston after more than a month at the Four Seasons Hotel.
France is set to play England in Miami for the World Cup third-place match after losing 2-0 to Spain.
The team practiced at Bentley University in Waltham while staying in Boston.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon's production company, Artists Equity, begins early development on a movie about Karen Read's controversial murder case.
Karen Read was acquitted of murder charges in 2022 regarding her boyfriend, Officer John O'Keefe's death in Canton, Massachusetts.
Several productions, including a Prime Video series, are also in development about Read's legal battle and subsequent lawsuits.

title: Steve Carell and his wife, Nancy, celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Steve Carell and Nancy Carell celebrated her upcoming 60th birthday at Tresca, a North End restaurant in Boston.
Tresca is co-owned by Bruins legend Ray Bourque, with a photo of the celebration posted on the restaurant's Instagram.
Nancy, born in Cohasset and a Boston College graduate, met Steve during an improv class he taught at Second City.

title: Some Saugus residents stunned by high water bills, with one nearing $200,000
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Saugus residents report unusually high water bills after a switch to new meters, with some bills nearing $200,000.
About a dozen homeowners have filed abatements; the town is reviewing whether the meter switch or systems are causing the issue.
Unpaid disputed water bills have resulted in property liens or funds pulled from mortgage escrows, affecting homeowners financially.

title: Review & setlist: Bob Dylan was spookily stellar on a smoky night in Boston
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bob Dylan performed at the Leader Bank Pavilion in Boston on July 16, 2026, delivering a set focused on his more recent work.
The 85-year-old artist's performance was clear, avoiding the mumbly vocals often criticized in his later years.
Dylan's set included five songs from 2020’s 'Rough and Rowdy Ways' and a few covers, wrapped up with 'I Shall Be Released.'

title: The middle seat will become a table on United’s newest Airbus jets
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines introduces a middle-seat-turned-table on new Airbus A321XLR aircraft, enhancing space in Economy Plus.
The table spans armrest to armrest, featuring a soft leather-like cover and two cup holders for passengers' convenience.
The new seating option will be available for sale later this year, with domestic flights starting this fall.

title: Original ‘Gilmore Girls’ house is on the market, and it’s not in Stars Hollow
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The original 'Gilmore Girls' house is for sale at 31 Victoria Ave. in Markham, Ontario, for $2.76 million USD.
The house was used in the pilot episode and is located northeast of Toronto, featuring four bedrooms and four bathrooms.
The property includes a 550-square-foot barn, originally a village bakery, with historic baking artifacts displayed on-site.

title: Longtime Maine soccer coach recognized by FIFA as ‘Unsung Hero’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Gary Newell, a longtime Maine soccer coach, was honored by FIFA as the World Cup's 'Unsung Hero' in Boston.
Newell received a standing ovation and video messages from former players and England's Jude Bellingham at the award presentation.
The award recognizes Newell's decades of volunteer work and impact on youth soccer development in his community.

title: Decades-old weeping willow collapses in Boston Public Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 68-year-old weeping willow collapsed in Boston's Public Garden, far exceeding its typical 30-50 year lifespan.
The tree was deemed structurally sound in routine inspections; no injuries occurred from the collapse.
Friends of the Public Garden regularly remove aging trees and replant in the spring to maintain the garden's landscape.
